title: PhD
biography: David Liu is an American chemical biologist who invented base editing and prime editing, revolutionary CRISPR-derived genome editing technologies that can precisely correct DNA mutations without double-strand breaks. His tools can address the majority of known pathogenic point mutations in humans.
aiSummary: David Liu is a chemical biology specialist with an H-index of 120 at Harvard and the Broad Institute. Invented base editing and prime editing for precision genome correction. Based in Cambridge, United States.
techniquesInvented: Base editing (cytosine and adenine base editors)

affiliations.role: Richard Merkin Professor of Chemistry and Chemical Biology

affiliations.hospitalName: Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard
affiliations.role: Director, Merkin Institute of Transformative Technologies

citations.title: Programmable base editing of A*T to G*C in genomic DNA without DNA cleavage
Search-and-replace genome editing without double-strand breaks or donor DNA
Programmable editing of a target base in genomic DNA without double-stranded DNA cleavage

timeline.title: Invented cytosine base editing
timeline.description: Created the first base editor that converts C-G base pairs to T-A without double-strand breaks

timeline.title: Invented adenine base editing
timeline.description: Developed adenine base editors that convert A-T to G-C pairs, covering the majority of disease mutations

timeline.title: Invented prime editing
timeline.description: Created 'search-and-replace' genome editing that can make all 12 types of point mutations plus insertions and deletions

timeline.title: Breakthrough Prize in Life Sciences
timeline.description: Awarded for the invention of base editing and prime editing
